more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Warriors set to participate at Unity Cup in the United Kingdom
more.description: The Zimbabwe senior men’s soccer team will travel to the United Kingdom in May for the Unity Cup, which will see them compete against Jamaica, India, and Nigeria. The tournament is scheduled to run from the 26th to the 30th of May in London. The Warriors will be marking their debut at the tournament, where [...] The post Warriors set to participate at Unity Cup in the United Kingdom appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: ZRP Denies Issuing Statement On Douglas Coltart Assault, Says Investigations Underway
more.description: ZRP Denies Issuing Statement On Douglas Coltart Assault Case But Confirms ‘Investigations Are Underway’ The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has denied issuing a circulating statement on the alleged assault of lawyer Douglas Coltart, saying the message shared widely on social media is false and misleading, while confirming that investigations into the incident are underway. more.title: Parliament says Constitutional Amendment Number 3 public hearings were ‘cordial and peaceful’
more.description: THE Parliament of Zimbabwe has described the just-ended public hearings to gather opinions on Constitutional Amendment Number 3 as “cordial and tranquil.” In a statement on X this weekend, Parliament extended its gratitude to citizens for their participation. “Parliament of Zimbabwe extends approbation to all Zimbabweans who participated physically during the just ended CAB3 Hearings. more.description: ZACC Distances Itself From Deadly Masvingo Road Accident Which Killed Mother And Her 5 Children The Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (ZACC) has distanced itself from the deadly Masvingo road accident, which killed a mother and her five children, following circulating social media claims that ZACC officers were involved in events leading to the tragedy. more.title: ZACC distances itself from horrific accident that killed six family members along Harare-Masvingo highway
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (ZACC) has denied any involvement in Thursday’s accident that killed six members of the same family along the Harare-Masvingo highway. According to ZACC Commissioner Kindness Paradza, the anti-graft body was concerned with a circulating video and audio “that wrongly implicates the Commission’s officers in the tragedy and confirms to the public [...] The post ZACC distances itself from horrific accident that killed six family members along Harare-Masvingo highway appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Zimbabwe expands grass-substrate technology to nine provinces
more.description: Zimbabwe has scaled up the adoption of a technology that uses specially bred, high-yield grass as a sustainable, versatile resource to produce edible mushrooms, livestock feed and bio-fertiliser in an effort to strengthen food security, enhance climate resilience and transform rural livelihoods.

more.title: Zimbabwe to host Australia for ODI series in September
more.description: Zimbabwe Cricket to host Australia national cricket team for 3 ODIs from Sept 15 in Harare, their first visit in over a decade.

more.title: ZAPU leader warns against ‘unscrupulous’ bid to strip Zimbabweans of hard-won rights
more.description: The Zimbabwe African People’s Union (ZAPU) leader, Sibangilizwe Nkomo, has issued a stern warning against attempts to erode fundamental constitutional rights, as opposition to the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 continues to mount.

more.title: Communities petition Parliament over destructive mining in Boterekwa, Shurugwi

more.title: Excessive Bank Charges Set To Be Slashed
more.description: Source: Excessive Bank Charges Set To Be Slashed ⋆ Pindula News Cabinet has approved sweeping cuts to the cost of licences, permits and fees in the financial services sector, to dismantle “hidden costs” and rebuild trust in formal banking. more.title: Zim seeks billions for energy infrastructure investments
more.description: Source: Zim seeks billions for energy infrastructure investments –Newsday Zimbabwe ZIMBABWE requires several billions to transform the country’s energy sector, with the bulk of the investment expected to be driven by private capital, Energy and Power Development minister July Moyo has indicated. more.title: Bulawayo water rationing remains in force
more.description: Source: Bulawayo water rationing remains in force -Newsday Zimbabwe BULAWAYO residents will continue to endure water-shedding for the foreseeable future, despite improving dam levels, as ageing and inadequate infrastructure cripples the city’s ability to supply sufficient water.
